items.question_text: Under 10-7-3, what avoidance minima must VFR aircraft be advised to maintain from a ground missile emergency location?
items.explanation: Per 10-7-3, VFR aircraft must be advised to avoid the emergency location by 1 mile laterally or 6,000 feet vertically, or by a greater distance or altitude when suggested by the notifying official.

choices.text: 1 mile laterally or 6,000 feet vertically, or greater when suggested by the notifying official.
3 miles laterally or 2,000 feet vertically.
5 miles laterally and 1,000 feet vertically — standard IFR separation minima.
VFR aircraft receive no specific avoidance minima; they are only advised of the location.

items.question_text: Under 10-7-4, what specific hazard must all aircraft be advised to avoid in the vicinity of a ground missile emergency?
items.explanation: Per 10-7-4, advise all aircraft to avoid any observed smoke columns in the vicinity of a ground missile emergency.

choices.text: Any observed smoke columns.
Any visible flame or fire on the ground.
The entire county or parish containing the emergency.
Only the launch pad or silo area, not any downwind effects.

items.question_text: Under 10-7-5, what may be issued when reports indicate that a ground missile emergency will exist for an extended period of time?
items.explanation: Per 10-7-5, when reports indicate the emergency will exist for an extended period of time, a Notice to Airmen may be issued.

choices.text: A Notice to Airmen (NOTAM).
An ALNOT covering the missile emergency area.
A Temporary Flight Restriction (TFR).
A controller-to-pilot broadcast every 15 minutes indefinitely.

items.question_text: When a ground missile emergency occurs, what are the en route and terminal notification responsibilities under 10-7-1?

choices.text: En route: notify air carrier company radio stations for VFR company aircraft in the vicinity and FSSs adjacent to the emergency. Terminal: relay all information to the ARTCC where the emergency exists and disseminate as a NOTAM.
Both en route and terminal facilities notify the RCC directly.
Only terminal facilities act — en route facilities have no ground missile emergency duties under this paragraph.
Both en route and terminal simply broadcast the alert on all frequencies with no further notification required.

items.explanation: Per 10-7-1, en route facilities notify air carrier company radio stations for VFR company aircraft in the vicinity and adjacent FSSs. Terminal facilities relay all information to the ARTCC where the emergency exists and disseminate as a NOTAM.

items.question_text: Under 10-7-2, what IFR and SVFR separation minima apply for rerouting aircraft around a ground missile emergency location?

choices.text: 1 mile lateral or 6,000 feet vertical, or greater minima when suggested by the notifying official.
3 miles lateral or 1,000 feet vertical, which are the standard IFR radar separation minima.
5 miles lateral and 2,000 feet vertical, because missile emergencies require double-normal separation.
There are no fixed minima — each emergency is handled case by case without reference to any standard separation distance.

items.explanation: Per 10-7-2, IFR and SVFR aircraft must be rerouted by 1 mile lateral separation or 6,000 feet vertical separation from the emergency location, or by greater minima when suggested by the notifying official.

items.question_text: How do the VFR avoidance minima for ground missile emergencies compare to the IFR/SVFR minima under 10-7-2 and 10-7-3?

choices.text: They are the same — 1 mile laterally and 6,000 feet vertically for both VFR and IFR/SVFR aircraft.
VFR minima are smaller because VFR aircraft have better visibility.
VFR aircraft have no specific minima; they are only told about the emergency location.
IFR/SVFR minima are 3 miles/1,000 feet while VFR minima are 1 mile/6,000 feet.

items.explanation: Per 10-7-2 and 10-7-3, the avoidance minima are the same for all categories: 1 mile lateral or 6,000 feet vertical, with the option for greater separation when suggested by the notifying official.

items.question_text: Under 10-7-4, which aircraft must be advised to avoid smoke columns near a ground missile emergency?

choices.text: All aircraft.
Only VFR aircraft operating below 6,000 feet.
Only IFR aircraft, because VFR aircraft can see and avoid smoke independently.
Only military aircraft, because they are the most likely to encounter missile-related smoke.

items.explanation: Per 10-7-4, advise all aircraft to avoid any observed smoke columns in the vicinity of a ground missile emergency.

items.question_text: Which facility type has the extended-notification responsibility for ground missile emergencies under 10-7-5?

choices.text: En route facilities — when reports indicate the emergency will exist for an extended period, a NOTAM may be issued.
Terminal facilities only — extended notification is a local tower responsibility.
Any facility that receives the initial emergency report may issue the extended NOTAM.
Only the RCC, because extended notification is a search-and-rescue function.

items.explanation: Per 10-7-5, the extended notification responsibility is designated EN ROUTE — when reports indicate an emergency will exist for an extended period of time, a Notice to Airmen may be issued.
